commit.message:
feat: overhaul to Supabase Auth (GoTrue) compatible drop-in replacement

- Replace Cargo.toml with new dependencies (dotenvy, jsonwebtoken, argon2, thiserror, tower-http cors, clap v4, base64 v0.22, rand v0.9)
- Add AppState with JWT config, instance ID, and DB pool
- Rewrite error module with typed AuthError enum and GoTrue-compatible JSON responses
- Add auth/jwt.rs with HS256 JWT encode/decode and Claims struct
- Add auth/password.rs with Argon2 password hashing and verification
- Rewrite model/mod.rs with User, UserResponse, TokenResponse, Session, RefreshToken
- Add middleware/auth.rs with AuthUser and AdminUser extractors
- Implement all GoTrue-compatible HTTP endpoints:
  - POST /signup - user registration with email/password
  - POST /token?grant_type=password|refresh_token - authentication and token refresh
  - POST /logout - session revocation (local/global/others scope)
  - GET/PUT /user - get and update current user
  - POST /verify - email confirmation and password recovery verification
  - POST /recover - initiate password recovery
  - POST /resend - resend confirmation/recovery emails
  - POST /otp, /magiclink - magic link / OTP flow
  - GET/POST /admin/users - admin user management
  - GET/PUT/DELETE /admin/users/:id - admin user CRUD
  - GET /health - health check with DB ping
  - GET /settings - server capability settings
- Rewrite main.rs to use environment-based config without clap CLI
- Add CORS, tracing, and timeout middleware via tower-http
- All unit tests pass (JWT encode/decode, password hashing, error responses)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add days/status to my_games, fix ACD2 cards, and add connection timeouts (#4564)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Corruption and Void Path text

Clarify timing and scope for two action cards. Change Corruption's trigger from "When a public objective is revealed" to "After a public objective is revealed" in acd2.json and legacy_data.json. Update Void Path in acd2.json to trigger "After you activate a system" and limit its wormhole effect to the current tactical action (previously applied "During this turn").

* Use HTTP client for image fetching and timeouts

Replace direct URL.openStream image reads with HttpClient-based requests to allow timeouts, a User-Agent header, and better error handling in ImageHelper.readImageURL. Add imports for HttpRequest/HttpResponse/Duration and StringUtils.isBlank, log non-200 responses and decode failures, and handle HttpTimeoutException and InterruptedException (preserving interrupt flag). Update EgressClientManager to build the shared HttpClient with a 10s connect timeout. Also apply a minor StringBuilder formatting refactor in SearchGameHelper (no behavioral change).

* Update ImageHelper.java

* Centralize HTTP client and add request timeouts

Use a shared EgressClientManager HTTP client in URLReaderHelper and add explicit timeouts for various HTTP requests (RestDiscordClient: 5s, AsyncTi4WebsiteHelper: 20s, UltimateStatisticsWebsiteHelper: 30s). Also tighten the image fetch timeout to 2s and remove the custom User-Agent header in ImageHelper. These changes standardize request behavior and help avoid long-running or hanging network calls.

commit.message:
feat(m2): thin end-to-end slice — engine, headless mode, game loop

* feat(m2): thin end-to-end slice — engine, headless mode, game loop

Implements M2 (cryptd-7uw): the minimal slice that proves the full
L1 architecture is wired up and runnable end-to-end without LLM.

New packages:
- internal/engine: deterministic rules engine (NewGame, Move, Look)
  with typed errors NoExitError and LockedError
- internal/interpreter: RulesInterpreter maps text commands to
  EngineActions (go <dir>, look, quit and aliases)
- internal/narrator: TemplateNarrator produces fixed-template
  narrations for all event types
- internal/renderer: CLIRenderer reads stdin / writes stdout
- internal/game: Loop wires engine+interpreter+narrator+renderer;
  drives full pipeline until quit or context cancel

CLI:
- crypt headless --scenario <id>: runs headless mode; respects
  CRYPT_SCENARIO_DIR env var for scenario lookup path

Tests:
- Unit tests in all packages (no build tag)
- Integration tests in internal/game with adversarial fakes covering
  ctx cancel, channel close, quit event, narrate/render error paths
- E2E test (build tag e2e) compiles binary and runs scripted session
- Coverage ≥ 90% on all M2 packages (engine 93.8%, game 92.1%,
  narrator/renderer 100%, interpreter 95%)

Fixtures:
- testdata/scripts/minimal-run.yaml: M2 acceptance script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(m2): address review — state corruption, quit narration, context, types

Addresses all issues surfaced by Cursor Bugbot review:

- engine: validate destination room before mutating GameState so a
  failed Move never leaves state corrupted (state-before-error-check)
- engine: hidden connections now return NoExitError (undiscoverable
  until revealed) instead of LockedError
- engine/doc.go: remove premature 'combat, inventory' claims
- game/loop: route renderer-emitted quit events through dispatch so
  quit narration ('Farewell, adventurer.') is rendered before exit
- game/loop: render quit narration after dispatch returns quit event
- game/loop: map LockedError -> 'locked_door' event, NoExitError ->
  'no_exit' event so narrators can give specific feedback
- narrator: add 'locked_door' and 'no_exit' template narrations
- renderer/cli: move stdin scan into goroutine (startOnce) so
  Render never blocks on input and ctx cancellation is honoured
- cmd/crypt: use filepath.Join for cross-platform path assembly
- cmd/crypt: rename usage strings from 'crypt' to 'cryptd'
- e2e: build binary as 'cryptd' matching documented binary name
- testdata/scripts: fix header comment (no --script flag exists)
- tests: remove unused errRenderer; add locked_door/no_exit/unknown
  dispatch coverage; use select+timeout in renderer tests

Coverage: engine 91.2%, game 97.6%, narrator/renderer 100% (total 91.6%)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(engine): hidden connections not leaked in exitList

exitList filtered over all room.Connections without checking type,
causing hidden connections to appear in LookResult.Exits and
MoveResult.Exits — contradicting the engine's own policy that hidden
connections are undiscoverable until revealed.

Now exitList skips entries where conn.Type == "hidden", consistent
with Move which already returns NoExitError for hidden directions.

Added tests:
- TestLook_HiddenConnectionsNotInExits: verifies north (hidden) absent
- TestMove_HiddenIsNoExit: verifies hidden direction returns NoExitError
Updated minimal.yaml: added secret_room with hidden north exit from entrance.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(game): propagate internal engine errors from dispatch instead of swallowing them as no_exit

NoExitError and LockedError are expected game conditions mapped to distinct
events. Any other error from Engine.Move indicates state/scenario corruption
and must propagate so callers can surface a diagnostic.

Added TestLoop_MoveInternalErrorPropagates to cover the new branch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(ci): automated issue triage and implementation workflow (#9)

Adds .github/workflows/issue-triage.yml which triggers on every new
issue opened. The workflow uses the GitHub Models API (o3-mini by default,
configurable via GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var) to:

- Classify the issue as bug, enhancement, or other and apply the
  corresponding label automatically
- Score difficulty (1-5) and impact (1-5) with short reasoning
- Route to one of three outcomes:
  * impact < 4 AND type=bug or type=enhancement  → label 'evaluation needed' + post evaluation summary
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty >= 4 → post a structured plan comment
  * impact >= 4 AND difficulty <= 3 → generate code, push a branch, open PR
  * type=other AND any impact  -> label 'evaluation needed'

No additional secrets are required — GITHUB_TOKEN covers both the
GitHub Models API and PR creation. The model can be swapped by changing
the GITHUB_MODELS_MODEL env var (e.g. gpt-4o, o1, gpt-4o-mini).

Allows the issue triage workflow to be run manually from the GitHub
Actions UI for any existing issue, in addition to triggering
automatically on newly opened issues.

Replace the static sourceFiles array in both the evaluate and
auto-implement steps with a recursive fs.readdirSync('lua') call.
This keeps codebase context up to date automatically as new Lua
modules are added without requiring workflow changes.

Previously only impact < 4 triggered the label. Now type=other always
gets 'evaluation needed' regardless of impact score, matching the
intended behaviour that unclassified issues always need manual review.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix `open` insertion position for nested explicit modules (issue #789) (#1495)

* Fix open insertion to place inside nearest enclosing module (issue #789)

FCS's TryFindInsertionContext sets Pos.Line to the module *declaration*
line (e.g. 'module Foo =') rather than the first line inside the module.
adjustInsertionPoint passed this value straight through for ScopeKind.Module,
which caused the generated 'open' to be inserted *before* the module
declaration instead of inside it.

Fix: detect when the candidate insertion line is an explicit module
declaration ('module X =') and advance by one so the open lands inside
the module.  This makes the code-fix behaviour consistent with code
completion (issue #789).

Also adds isExplicitModuleDeclaration helper and a regression test.

commit.message:
Add ARM32 (armv7/thumbv7) thread-local dispatch for parallel test execution (#114)

* Add ARM32 (armv7/thumbv7) thread-local dispatch for parallel test support

Implement thread-local dispatch for ARM32 architecture, matching the
existing x86_64 and aarch64 implementations. This enables parallel test
execution on ARM32 Linux targets by replacing the global mutex approach
with per-thread function replacement routing.

Changes:
- Add target_arch = "arm" to all thread-local dispatch cfg gates across
  thread_local_registry.rs, internal.rs, injector.rs, common.rs, and
  thread_safety.rs
- Implement ARM32 dispatcher JIT code generation (ARM mode) with
  register save/restore for both integer (r0-r3) and VFP (d0-d7) regs
- Add ARM32 trampoline creation with support for both ARM and Thumb mode
  jump-back sequences
- Add PC-relative instruction fixup for ARM mode trampolines
- Add ARM32 branch patch generation matching existing patch_arm.rs format
  including Thumb alignment handling
- Add ARM32 JIT code for boolean return values (MOV r0, #imm; BX lr)
- Enable allocate_jit_memory for ARM32 (uses simple mmap fallback)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ARM32 dispatcher stack alignment and patch register clobbering

- Fix AAPCS stack alignment violation: PUSH {r0-r3, r4, lr} (24 bytes,
  8-aligned) instead of PUSH {r0-r3, lr} (20 bytes, misaligned).
  Misaligned SP before BLX caused SIGABRT on armv7.

- Use r12 (IP, scratch register) instead of r7 (callee-saved/frame pointer)
  for Thumb branch patch. r7 clobbering corrupted the caller's frame pointer,
  causing SIGSEGV on thumbv7. Uses MOVW/MOVT r12 encoding which is also
  alignment-independent.

- Use r12 instead of r9 (callee-saved) for ARM branch patch.

* Reduce ARM32 test stress and add diagnostics for CI

- Add RUST_BACKTRACE=full for ARM target CI runs
- Run ARM tests with --test-threads=1 to avoid resource exhaustion
  on 32-bit compat runners (armv7 was hitting thread spawn limits)
- Reduce stress test THREAD_COUNT from 20 to 4 on ARM32

* Fix ARM32 function overflow with dynamic 4-byte patch size

Use dynamic patch sizing for ARM32: 4-byte B.W (Thumb) or B (ARM) when
the dispatcher is within branch range (±16MB/±32MB), falling back to
12-byte MOVW/MOVT/BX when it is not. This eliminates the previous
12-byte patch overflow into adjacent small functions that caused
SIGSEGV on thumbv7.

Key changes:
- Add ARM32 to constrained JIT allocation (±16MB range) in common.rs
- Pre-allocate dispatcher, then choose patch size based on distance
- For Thumb 4-byte patches, prepend a Thumb-to-ARM stub (LDR.W/BX)
  since Thumb B.W cannot switch processor mode
- Encode Thumb B.W (T4) and ARM B (unconditional) for 4-byte patches
- Add -j 1 to CI cargo test for ARM targets to prevent resource
  exhaustion from parallel test binary execution

* Add diagnostics for thumbv7 SIGSEGV in test_will_execute_raw_closure_per_thread

Add eprintln diagnostics to:
- install_dispatcher_arm32: log addresses, distance, patch size
- generate_branch_patch_arm32: verify B.W encoding round-trips correctly
- test_will_execute_raw_closure_per_thread: log each step to identify crash point

Also add isolated test run CI step for ARM targets to determine if the
crash is caused by accumulated state from prior tests.

* Fix ARM32 Thumb overlapping patch SIGILL and instruction boundary bugs

- Use std::hint::black_box in test helper functions to ensure minimum
  4-byte code size on Thumb (prevents compiler from generating 2-byte
  functions that cause overlapping patches)
- Add calculate_thumb_copy_size() to scan Thumb instructions and find
  the first complete instruction boundary >= patch_size bytes, preventing
  the trampoline from splitting a 32-bit Thumb instruction
- Add check_arm32_patch_overlap() to detect overlapping patches at
  runtime with clear diagnostic messages (only checks active patches
  with ref_count > 0)
- Remove diagnostic eprintln statements and isolated test CI step
